Northern Beaches Painting

--- Our Services ---
[vc_row css=. vc_custom_1726898725370{margin-top: -50px. important;}][vc_column][vc_column_text] [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=2/3][vc_empty_space height=62px][vc_column_text] Residential Painting Services We take pride in enhancing and personalising your home: to preserve and improve your investment and sanctuary.

Whether it’s interior or exterior painting that’s required, Northern Beaches Painting paint and treat all types of spaces—from small touch ups, art deco, heritage, luxury homes, new builds and renovations—working with you to choose the product, brand and colour, to the beautiful finish you desire.

[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_empty_space height=62px][vc_column_text] Commercial Painting Services No job is too large or small—whether commercial, industrial, interior and exterior we have a specialised team, highly skilled in managing the complexities of commercial painting.

Health and Safety Our health and safety record is excellent, we follow OHS procedures and keep our employees informed of requirements. One stop shop For clients’ convenience we are happy to obtain quotations and manage the work of other third party suppliers i. e. scaffolders, carpenters and plasterers.

Free detailed quotation We promise to provide a free quotation within 48 hours of visiting the project site. The quotation specifies the work to be undertaken, preparation and materials. Qualified and insured All our employees are qualified, fully licensed and insured for $5 million in public liability. Advice We will advise you on technical and aesthetical aspects of the job; recommending paints, treatments and materials. We only use premium quality products.

[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text css=] Learn more about our business and our professional painting services About our business Our Services Our Portfolio [/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=] We only use trusted products [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- Carpentry Handyman Services ---
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] Carpentry & Handyman Services We can help you with your dream backyard renovation projects.

We specialise in building timber decking and steps, creating a rustic outdoor shower and custom made stylish outdoor kitchen – complete with timber doors, concrete top, ceramic splash back and BBQ. Let us take care of your handyman check list – from architraves to gyprocking, replacing fascia boards, hanging paintings, putting up shelves to fixing fences. Check out our projects and get in touch to discuss your renovation and handyman projects.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/3][vc_empty_space height=62px][vc_empty_space height=62px][vc_column_text] Special interior effects Wallpapering – removal and hanging Rag rolling Sponging Suede effect [/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text] Our Projects Commercial aged care facilities Strata Apartment Blocks Warehouses Factories Universities Retailers [/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=] Our commitment to you Preparation We always ensure that the surface is well prepared first by washing down thoroughly, removing mould as required and advising on the recommended method of treatment.

100% customer satisfaction guarantee We will turn up on time, deliver the job on budget, treat your property with respect, leaving it clean and tidy and inspect the job continually for quality control. If you’re not happy, we’re not happy. Environment We are mindful of the environment and our customers’ health and safety. We strive to use environmentally friendly products where possible, disposing of paints properly.

As principal contractors for Lend Lease and Salvation Army’s aged care facilities, strata management and real estate companies, we are proud to contribute to the ongoing modernisation of local buildings, businesses and homes. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_empty_space height=32][vc_column_text css=] Carpentry & Handyman Services We can help you with your dream backyard renovation projects.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- Thank you for contacting us ---

[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]We would love to talk to you about your painting and decorating project. Please complete the form below or contact us by email or phone us on 0416 180 014 for a free consultation and no obligation quotation.

--- Home ---
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_empty_space height=50px][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_column_text css=] Welcome to Northern Beaches Painting Northern Beaches Painting is owned by Philip Torrance.

Phillip and his team moved in, quickly and efficiently completing the transformation of the large area to look like new. We are more than happy to now look forward to the exteriors being given the same professional treatment by this company.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_gallery type=image_grid images=2077,2076,2074,2075,2078,2073 img_size=350x title=Photo Gallery][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=] Learn more about our business and our professional painting services About our business Our Services Our Portfolio [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=] What our clients think about us Our home at Whale Beach was given a new life when Northern Beaches Painting repainted all internal surfaces last month.

Katrina Chandler - Whale Beach, NSW [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column][/vc_column][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- Colour therapy for dementia ---
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][vc_column_text] Northern Beaches Painting were tasked with painting a leading residential aged care facility in Sydney for one of their valued commercial clients, Elizabeth Jenkins Place Aged Care Plus Centre, a division of The Salvation Army.

Katrina Chandler - Whale Beach, NSW [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- Portfolio ---
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space height=50px][vc_column_text] We take pride in the quality of work we do Northern Beaches Painting’s vision is to provide high quality painting and decorating and excellent customer service, which exceed clients’ expectations. Below are some examples of completed projects.

Phillip and his team moved in, quickly and efficiently completing the transformation of the large area to look like new. We are more than happy to now look forward to the exteriors being given the same professional treatment by this company.

With over 20 years’ experience in the trade, he has built a reputable business with established clients, based on Sydney’s northern beaches. His team undertake the full scope of residential painting, plus carpentry and handyman jobs. Projects include architecturally designed homes featured in Grand Designs magazine, and working with Lend Lease and Salvation Army’s aged care facilities.

We specialise in building timber decking and steps, creating a rustic outdoor shower and custom made stylish outdoor kitchen complete with timber doors, concrete top, ceramic splash back and BBQ. Let us take care of your handyman check list from architraves to gyprocking, replacing fascia boards, hanging paintings, putting up shelves to fixing fences. Check out our projects and get in touch to discuss your renovation and handyman projects.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_gallery type=image_grid images=1883,1884,1887,1888,1893,1894,1885,1892,1889,1891,1886,1890,1881,1882,1879,1880][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] Contact Northern Beaches Painting on northernbeachespainting@gmail. com or 0416 180 014 for a free quotation.

Catering for both men and women is also required, while women favour prettier colours these may not appeal to men. Green is considered the most relaxing of colours, reducing activity in the central nervous system, and is therefore suitable for bedrooms, and sleep. In contrast, red is stimulating, increasing brain activity and raising the perceived room temperature.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- Kangaroo Valley dream home ---
[vc_row][vc_column][rev_slider_vc alias=kangaroo-valley][vc_column_text]Set on 10 acres of rolling hills in Kangaroo Valley, NSW, this breathtaking property is one of the most beautiful and challenging projects Northern Beaches Painting are proud to have been involved in. The building was designed by Sydney architect Duncan Sanby and featured in Grand Design magazine June 2016.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row full_width=stretch_row_content_no_spaces seperator_indeed_locker= lk_t=ism_template_1 lk_io=default lk_dm=0 lk_thm=0 lk_tuo=0 ru_on=0 uhc_on=0 ur_on=0 ref_on=0][vc_column][vc_single_image image=1922 img_size=full][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]Fascinated about the vivid block colour scheme that had been chosen, we discovered an interesting heart warming story about how the use of certain colours benefits people living with dementia, helping to create a supportive environment and improving their physical, mental and emotional wellbeing.

Painting the walls a different colour to the floor and stair colour is helpful for their spatial awareness. As people living with dementia tend to look up, they don’t see the differentiation between carpets and steps, walls and floor, particularly if all surfaces are painted the same colour. Colour use for people living with dementia includes considering which colour is suitable for each room, depending on how and who uses the space.

Research from the UK indicates that older people’s vision changes and with or without people living with dementia perceive colour differently. DSDC The Dementia Centre advises: The thickening and yellowing of the lens alters the way colour is perceived. As a result older people experience a reduction in contrast perception ability, resulting in difficulty differentiating between subtle changes in the environment such as carpets and steps.

This is important for elderly people who tend to feel the cold and are often unable to regulate their body temperature, although red tends to be perceived as pink for dementia sufferers. As DSDC The Dementia Centre indicates there’s an “altered perception of colour” with dementia sufferers being “less able to discriminate between unsaturated (less intense) colours such as pastels.

Todd also praised Northern Beaches Painting on the work undertaken, saying, We at Elizabeth Jenkins Place Aged Care Plus Centre couldnt be more happy with the service Northern Beaches Painting provide. Competitive quotes and great work. On a personal level it is hard to find contractors that you can trust to get the job done and are able to leave unsupervised. Northern Beaches Painting ticks all the boxes for me.

Duncan Sanby took the design inspiration from agricultural structures. “ The building was designed to be subservient to the landscape and semi-industrial in appearance,” Duncan Sanby We painted both interior and exterior but the main project was interior. Spanning three spacious wings including four bedrooms, huge living kitchen dining area, TV room and study, it was no mean feat to paint. The colour palette is classic contemporary.

”[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]Todd Stoneley, Maintenance Supervisor, Elizabeth Jenkins Place Aged Care Plus Centre commented that he had observed a difference in residents mood and behaviour as soon as paint went on the walls. It has really made a positive difference.

We love Dulux and Taubmans for their modern colour range and quality finish. Remember bargain paints can produce disappointing results. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_text_separator title=To prime or not to prime.

color=black][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/4][vc_single_image image=1768 img_size=400300][/vc_column][vc_column width=3/4][vc_column_text]Use primer if: You’re painting a light colour over a dark one The walls are heavily marked The walls are patchy In these cases a primer will help create a consistent, neutral surface that your paint will stick to evenly[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_text_separator title=What is the order of events.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- How to paint like a professional ---
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][vc_column_text] You might think a lick of paint is easy to achieve but like most things, creating a perfect finish takes time and practice. These professional tips will help you create a smooth and polished look. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_text_separator title=Which paint.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1836 img_size=full][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1837 img_size=full][vc_single_image image=1835 img_size=full][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] Contact Northern Beaches Painting on northernbeachespainting@gmail. com or 0416 180 014 for a free quotation and colour consultation.

We painted all ceilings, doors and walls in Dulux vivid white to complement the simple industrial style blended with Scandinavian influence, and to reflect the abundance of light bouncing off the concrete floor and huge windows. The preparation was a job in itself, requiring the meticulous cutting in of white paint to black window frames and steelwork, which reached up to the apex of soaring 4. 5 metre-high ceilings.

color=black][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/4][vc_single_image image=1767 img_size=400300][/vc_column][vc_column width=3/4][vc_column_text] Clean walls, woodwork and ceilings Apply masking tape Lay down drop cloths Cut your corners Paint the room top to bottom: ceiling, then walls, then trim [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_text_separator title=What About the Ceiling.

We recommend you use water based paint, here are the reasons why: It dries faster It has low odour It gives plenty of sheen It’s easy to wipe clean [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_text_separator title=Paint Finishes color=black][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/4][vc_single_image image=1514 img_size=400300][/vc_column][vc_column width=3/4][vc_column_text]For paint finishes we recommend the following: For walls use low sheen or matte finish For the trim use semi gloss enamel or semi gloss water based enamel for a modern finish When buying paint, ensure you stick to good quality brands.

color=black][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/4][vc_single_image image=1769 img_size=400300][/vc_column][vc_column width=3/4][vc_column_text] Use a paint made specifically for ceilings. Ceiling paint is thicker than standard paint and extra matte so it hides imperfections Always paint the ceiling white. White paint makes ceilings appear whiter and brighter than they actually are.

color=black][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/4][vc_single_image image=1770 img_size=400300][/vc_column][vc_column width=3/4][vc_column_text]There are 2 types of paint, oil based and water based. These days there is no need to use oil based when painting a room. Oil based paint although durable can yellow over time and has a strong odour.

The effect is one of simple elegance, presenting a perfect canvas to feature the expansive views. Surrounded by forests, the only neighbours we met whilst working on this secluded retreat were the resident kangaroos and wombats, and the only sounds we heard were the songs of native birds.
Contact Northern Beaches Painting on northernbeachespainting@gmail. com or 0416 180 014 for a free quotation and colour consultation.
com or 0416 180 014 for a free quotation and colour consultation.

Check out eBay for bargains If using stone, ask around for quotations as pricing can vary [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1724 img_size=400300][vc_column_text] Give the entrance a facelift First impressions matter so make sure the entrance to your home has the WOW factor.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1705 img_size=400300][vc_column_text] Refresh with paint A fresh lick of paint is proven to have the most effect on your home. Given most people repaint every five years its worth calling in the experts for a professional job that will last. Woodwork and ceilings are trickier to get perfect and if youre preparing for sale potential buyers will notice those unsightly runs and patches.

Use high gloss paint and a grout pen which paints over discoloured grout [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1706 img_size=400300][vc_column_text] Make your windows sparkle Clean the windows until sparkling Check latches are working Call a glazier to replace damaged window panes Change your curtains or blinds keep it neutral if selling Ensure window frames are clean and in top condition, a fresh coat of paint works wonders [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1713 img_size=400300][vc_column_text] Spruce up the garden Have a good clear up, sweep pathways, remove debris and think about styling Group your pots together and give plants a good haircut, removing dead leave Deep clean paintwork and paths with a high pressure cleaner Scrub the barbecue and buy a new cover Clean and oil outdoor furniture [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][vc_column_text] Contact Northern Beaches Painting on northernbeachespainting@gmail.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1703 img_size=400300][vc_column_text] Fix up the kitchen For a quick update paint the cupboard doors An easy fix is to replace the cupboard doors and handles Replace your bench-top to change the look of the kitchen.

Paint the front door in a strong colour to give it extra bling Update your entrance hall furniture including the doormat Clean the area around the entrance hall Remove cobwebs from the eaves Tidy up plants and clean the path [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=1700 img_size=400300][vc_column_text] Bathroom bling Ensure everything is in good working order including toilets, taps and extractor fans Change your tapware for an instant update Paint your tiles, an effective yet economical option.

Poor preparation is false economy and if not done properly the paint will not stick, last or look as good. That said, if you are on a budget you can save a little on your painting budget by washing the areas down thoroughly yourself then getting the rest done professionally.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] We only use trusted products [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- Tips to add value to your home this Spring ---
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][vc_column_text] Whether you are thinking of selling this Spring or simply want to refresh your space, these tips will help to increase the value of your home.

Always roll the shortest length of the ceiling, roll away from where you’re standing, then zigzag back towards yourself, working in small, overlapping sections [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space][vc_column_text] Don’t have the time or patience for a DIY paint job. Get a quote from the professionals at Northern Beaches Painting who have perfected their craft over 20 years. Youre guaranteed a quality finish with professional and friendly customer service.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] We only use trusted products [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

--- About our business ---
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_empty_space][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_column_text css=] Philip Torrance Director Originally from England, Philip trained in ‘old school’ painting and decorating, serving a three-year apprenticeship to advanced stage with City and Guilds in London.

With a rigorous eye for detailed finishes, he specialises in premium residential properties, having painted heritage buildings in England and some of Sydney’s most prestigious homes. When he’s not painting Philip loves to cycle. He extends an uncompromising demand for high standards to his fitness and performance on the bike. In 2013 he realised his dream of ascending the mighty mountain, Alpe d’Huez at the Tour de France centurion.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=] Our values; We will treat your home or premises as if it were our own; completing the job with professionalism and meticulous detail. We guarantee superior painting results and will leave your home as we find it; clean and tidy.

As principal contractors for Lend Lease and Salvation Army’s aged care facilities, strata management and real estate companies, we are proud to contribute to the ongoing modernisation of local buildings, businesses and homes. Ben Taylor - Willoughby, NSW [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

Phil was even understanding on the extra money required to get the job done. Since then we have used Phil for further outside work and also for work conducted at my business too. I'd recommend him to any person looking for a higher quality and professional finish.

Green is considered the most relaxing of colours, reducing activity in the central nervous system, and is therefore suitable for bedrooms, and sleep. In contrast, red is stimulating, increasing brain activity and raising the perceived room temperature. This is important for elderly people who tend to feel the cold and are often unable to regulate their body temperature, although red tends to be perceived as pink for dementia sufferers.

--- Colour therapy for dementia - Northern Beaches Painting ---
Home Services Painting Carpentry Handyman Contact Portfolio About Featured News Colour therapy for dementia Northern Beaches Painting were tasked with painting a leading residential aged care facility in Sydney for one of their valued commercial clients, Elizabeth Jenkins Place Aged Care Plus Centre, a division of The Salvation Army.

Fascinated about the vivid block colour scheme that had been chosen, we discovered an interesting heartwarming story about how the use of certain colours benefits dementia sufferers, helping to create a supportive environment and improving their physical, mental and emotional wellbeing.

As people living with dementia tend to look up, they don’t see the differentiation between carpets and steps, walls and floor, particularly if all surfaces are painted the same colour. Colour use for people living with dementia includes considering which colour is suitable for each room, depending on how and who uses the space. Catering for both men and women is also required, while women favour prettier colours these may not appeal to men.

Fascinated about the vivid block colour scheme that had been chosen, we discovered an interesting heart warming story about how the use of certain colours benefits people living with dementia, helping to create a supportive environment and improving their physical, mental and emotional wellbeing. Research from the UK indicates that older people’s vision changes and with or without people living with dementia perceive colour differently.

DSDC The Dementia Centre advises: The thickening and yellowing of the lens alters the way colour is perceived. As a result older people experience a reduction in contrast perception ability, resulting in difficulty differentiating between subtle changes in the environment such as carpets and steps. Painting the walls a different colour to the floor and stair colour is helpful for their spatial awareness.

As DSDC The Dementia Centre indicates there’s an “altered perception of colour” with dementia sufferers being “less able to discriminate between unsaturated (less intense) colours such as pastels. ” Todd Stoneley, Maintenance Supervisor, Elizabeth Jenkins Place Aged Care Plus Centre commented that he had observed a difference in residents mood and behaviour as soon as paint went on the walls. It has really made a positive difference.

The colour palette is classic contemporary. We painted all ceilings, doors and walls in Dulux vivid white to complement the simple industrial style blended with Scandinavian influence, and to reflect the abundance of light bouncing off the concrete floor and huge windows. The preparation was a job in itself, requiring the meticulous cutting in of white paint to black window frames and steelwork, which reached up to the apex of soaring 4. 5 metre-high ceilings.

Todd also praised Northern Beaches Painting on the work undertaken, saying, We at Elizabeth Jenkins Place Aged Care Plus Centre couldnt be more happy with the service Northern Beaches Painting provide. Competitive quotes and great work. On a personal level it is hard to find contractors that you can trust to get the job done and are able to leave unsupervised. Northern Beaches Painting ticks all the boxes for me. Contact Northern Beaches Painting on northernbeachespainting@gmail.

These professional tips will help you create a smooth and polished look. Which paint. There are 2 types of paint, oil based and water based. These days there is no need to use oil based when painting a room. Oil based paint although durable can yellow over time and has a strong odour.

Remember bargain paints can produce disappointing results. To prime or not to prime. Use primer if: You’re painting a light colour over a dark one The walls are heavily marked The walls are patchy In these cases a primer will help create a consistent, neutral surface that your paint will stick to evenly What is the order of events.

Use a paint made specifically for ceilings. Ceiling paint is thicker than standard paint and extra matte so it hides imperfections Always paint the ceiling white. White paint makes ceilings appear whiter and brighter than they actually are.

We recommend you use water based paint, here are the reasons why: It dries faster It has low odour It gives plenty of sheen It’s easy to wipe clean Paint Finishes For paint finishes we recommend the following: For walls use low sheen or matte finish For the trim use semi gloss enamel or semi gloss water based enamel for a modern finish When buying paint, ensure you stick to good quality brands.
